text:
REGIME — Yield-driven risk-on, defensive/growth mix, metals lagged.

INDICES — VIX 24.2, falling. QQQ +0.60%, IWM +0.43%, SPY +0.47%, DIA +0.37% — large caps led small caps narrowly. VIXY dropped -2.45%, the sharpest cross-asset move, confirming the vol compression bid.

SECTORS — XLP +0.94%, XLY +0.82%, XLE +0.73%, XLF +0.71%, XLK +0.58%, XLI +0.51%, XLRE +0.36%, XLC +0.05%, XLV -0.36%, XLU -0.37%, XLB -0.38%. Top industries: MSOS +6.23%, IBIT +4.08%. Bottom: TAN -1.26%, LIT -1.42%.

THE 7s — Defense +1.25%, Fab +1.22%, Housing +1.08%, Toll Road +1.00%, Semi +0.84%, Bank +0.73%, Industrial +0.66%, Energy +0.57%, New Staples +0.51%, Old Staples +0.45%, Insurance +0.44%, Freight +0.35%, Cloud +0.19%, Old Metals +0.05%, Medtech -0.16%, Power -0.27%, Health -0.39%, Buildout -0.40%, Software -0.63%, New Metals -0.85%. Strongest ticker in Defense 7 not specified in data; weakest in New Metals 7 not individually named.

SIGNALS — Fear & Greed 54.4, Neutral. Top movers: MSOS +6.23% (Industries), IBIT +4.08% (Industries), EATZ +2.02% (Industries, 10.4x vol). 20d momentum: best sector XLE +6.19%, worst XLC -4.55%; best basket Energy +8.57%, worst Software -10.72%. Eli Lilly's +3% gain on its $7.8B Centessa acquisition drove XLV outperformance at the stock level despite the sector finishing -0.36%.

WATCH — EATZ today +2.02%, 10.4x average volume — unusual surge warrants confirmation or fade setup. IJK +0.28% on 8.0x vol spike with minimal price move — divergence suggests positioning, not news. XLC +0.05% badly lagged peers despite lower yields and Meta +3% — sector-level drag deserves scrutiny for tomorrow's open.
